title: oxalis parvifolia subsp. aureoflava
intro: Oxalis parvifolia subsp. aureoflava is a subspecies of the Oxalis parvifolia plant, commonly known as the small-leaved wood-sorrel. It is a perennial plant native to southern Africa, characterized by its yellow flowers and small, rounded leaves. The medicinal properties of Oxalis parvifolia subsp. aureoflava are attributed to its high content of oxalic acid and other compounds, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The plant is used in herbal preparations to support digestive health and treat conditions such as diarrhea and urinary tract infections, and it is also valued for its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
